publicly_supported_fact: BSI later stated that it conducted IT-system maintenance as a risk-mitigation step on 7 May.

BSI stated that customers experienced difficulty accessing services on 8 May and that normalization proceeded in stages; the year-end filing records that operations were affected by a cyber incident on this date.

event_title: Branch and ATM access restored in stages
publicly_supported_fact: BSI stated that branch and ATM transactions were available and basic BSI Mobile functions began returning gradually on 9 May.
BSI reported branch, ATM, and mobile-banking services as normal and stated that capacity for core banking and critical channels had been increased.
BSI stated that it found indications of a cyberattack, temporarily switched off several channels, and required audit and digital forensics for further proof.
OJK stated that services had become normal in stages, continued supervisory coordination, and requested acceleration of the forensic audit.
Reuters reported LockBit's claim concerning customer records; BSI did not confirm a data leak and stated that recovery, audit, and mitigation work was continuing.
